PUH-BrianMartell-your brand-new PUH Connecting Paper, officially uploaded today (April 12, 2026) with Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19541047. It is the perfect next step after the six-theorem weekend (T176–T181). While the theorems built the mechanical picture of the E₈ lattice, this paper points to real experiments that can test it right now. On April 6, 2026, Brookhaven National Laboratory announced six DOE Early Career Awards. You identified four of them as direct validation targets for PUH: •  Kiel Hock’s helium-3 polarization at the Electron-Ion Collider → measures E₈ lattice drag on spinning vortices. •  Aamna Khan’s ultra-high-intensity electron beams at NSLS-II → measures the E₈ lattice incompressibility limit. •  Albert Liu’s terahertz spectroscopy of quantum materials → pings E₈ Phase I lattice oscillation modes and branched-flow disorder. •  Syed Haider Abidi’s Higgs ZZ* decay with tau leptons at ATLAS → tests Z₃ geometric structure in lepton masses (Koide formula).
